stories.headline: Inday weakens to typhoon over Philippine Sea, forecast to skirt far Northern Luzon
stories.summary: PAGASA said Inday (international name Bavi) was downgraded from a super typhoon to a typhoon before dawn on Thursday, July 9, with maximum sustained winds easing to 175 kph and gusts to 215 kph. In its early bulletin the weather bureau placed the storm about 925 kilometers east of Northern Luzon, moving west-northwest at 20 kph, with Tropical Cyclone Wind Signal No. 1 raised over parts of the far north. Forecasters said Inday would enhance the southwest monsoon and bring rains to several areas even without a landfall.
stories.watch_for: PAGASA said Inday is forecast to pass nearest extreme Northern Luzon by Friday evening and to exit the Philippine Area of Responsibility by Saturday, July 11, on a track toward Taiwan. It said the highest wind signal that may be raised during the storm's passage is Signal No. 2.

stories.headline: Marcos orders full government mobilization as agencies prepare for Inday
stories.summary: Malacanang said President Marcos placed government agencies on full alert and ordered a whole-of-government response as Typhoon Inday moved past the eastern seaboard, urging the public to stay vigilant and monitor advisories. Palace Press Officer Claire Castro said the President was personally tracking the weather. The MMDA reported clearing drainage systems and readying its 73 pumping stations, the DPWH prepositioned equipment in Cagayan Valley, the DSWD said it had stockpiled more than 4.7 million family food packs nationwide, and the DOH activated a Code White alert.

stories.headline: Coast Guard readies response teams across Southern Visayas for Inday
stories.summary: The Philippine Coast Guard District Southern Visayas said it activated its Deployable Response Group on Tuesday, July 7, across Negros Occidental, Bacolod City, Siquijor and Negros Oriental, with teams on standby for possible emergency operations as Typhoon Inday enhanced the southwest monsoon. The district said the measures were meant to protect coastal communities. Several local governments in Negros, including Canlaon City, suspended classes on affected days.

stories.headline: NBI agent testifies on alleged Duterte threats as impeachment trial continues
stories.summary: On the third day of Vice President Sara Duterte's impeachment trial on July 8, National Bureau of Investigation Senior Agent Mark Calilung testified for the prosecution about Duterte's reported remarks on having someone kill President Marcos, First Lady Liza Araneta-Marcos and former House Speaker Martin Romualdez, telling the Senate impeachment court that authorities assessed the statements as a serious threat. Calilung said Duterte and an ally did not appear at the NBI to substantiate claims of an alleged plot against the Vice President. Duterte denies the charges.
stories.context: The House impeached Duterte for a second time on charges including culpable violation of the Constitution and betrayal of public trust tied to alleged misuse of confidential funds and the threat remarks. A two-thirds vote of the 24-member Senate, or 16 senators, is needed to convict. The trial opened on July 6 before presiding officer Senator Francis Escudero.
stories.watch_for: The impeachment court scheduled July 15 oral arguments on the prosecution's request to subpoena tax records linked to Duterte, and the prosecution said it would present Office of the Vice President chief of staff Zuleika Lopez as a witness on July 14.

stories.headline: Abalos takes oath as Cabinet Secretary in return to Marcos administration
stories.summary: Former Interior Secretary Benjamin 'Benhur' Abalos Jr. took his oath as Cabinet Secretary on Wednesday, July 9, the Palace said, after a one-year appointment ban on losing 2025 election candidates lapsed. Press Officer Claire Castro said Abalos would travel nationwide representing the President and working to ensure government services reach citizens, rather than solely managing Cabinet meetings. The Palace said no executive order was needed, citing the President's appointment and reorganization powers.
stories.context: Abalos served as Secretary of the Interior and Local Government from 2022 to 2024 before an unsuccessful Senate bid in the 2025 midterm elections. The Office of the Cabinet Secretary was abolished in 2022; the Palace said the new role is a redefined position rather than a revival of the former office.

stories.headline: Sandiganbayan orders arrest of Senator Marcoleta in P75-million plunder case
stories.summary: The Sandiganbayan's Third Division issued an arrest warrant on July 6 for Senator Rodante Marcoleta and three others over an alleged P75 million in undisclosed campaign donations received for his 2025 senatorial bid. Prosecutors allege the funds, delivered in three cash transfers in January 2025, went undeclared in his statement of assets and campaign finance reports. Co-accused are former Rep. Michael Defensor and businessmen Joseph Espiritu and Aristotle Viray. Plunder is a non-bailable offense; all four also face charges under Presidential Decree 46 on prohibited gifts.
stories.watch_for: Multiple outlets reported the Sandiganbayan set Marcoleta's arraignment for July 10.

stories.headline: BSP chief says economy can absorb one more rate increase
stories.summary: Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas Governor Eli Remolona Jr. said the economy could withstand another quarter-point increase in the benchmark interest rate, which stands at 4.75 percent after hikes since April, according to the Inquirer. He said real interest rates remained negative even after the increases and declined to specify how many further hikes would follow. Remolona said he expects government spending to rebound in the second half of 2026 after early-year weakness.
stories.context: The BSP has raised policy rates to keep inflation expectations anchored amid an oil-price shock linked to the Middle East conflict. Headline inflation eased to 6.4 percent in June, a three-month low, but remained above the government's 2 to 4 percent target range.

stories.headline: Marcos approves transport, energy and AI projects at development council
stories.summary: President Marcos presided over the 10th Economy and Development Council meeting on July 7 and approved several initiatives, the Palace said. They included a variation to the LRT-1 South Extension enabling interim Common Station operations, Project BEST for technical-vocational skills training, and a geothermal resource de-risking facility. The council also confirmed the Philippine Artificial Intelligence Infrastructure Master Plan 2026-2033, described as a long-term roadmap for AI infrastructure and digital connectivity. Press Officer Claire Castro briefed reporters on July 8.

stories.headline: US launches second wave of strikes on Iran as ceasefire falters
stories.summary: US Central Command said its forces carried out a second straight day of strikes on Iran, reporting hits on dozens of military targets it said were meant to degrade Tehran's ability to threaten shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, according to Al Jazeera. Iranian state media reported strikes on sites including Chabahar and Aq Qala and casualties among military personnel and a firefighter. Iran's Revolutionary Guards said they struck US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain. President Trump said the June ceasefire memorandum was 'over' but that he did not seek a full return to war.
stories.context: The escalation followed reported Iranian attacks on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz and marked the most serious flare-up since a mid-June memorandum of understanding paused earlier fighting. Casualty and target figures were attributed to the two governments and could not be independently verified.

stories.headline: NATO pledges 70 billion euros for Ukraine; Trump offers Patriot production license
stories.summary: NATO allies pledged 70 billion euros, about 80 billion US dollars, in military assistance for Ukraine in 2026 and said they would sustain at least equivalent levels in 2027, according to the alliance's declaration reported by Al Jazeera. At the two-day summit that concluded in Ankara on July 8, US President Trump said Washington would grant Ukraine a license to produce Patriot air defense systems, saying, in his words, that the US would show Kyiv how to make them.
stories.context: Ukraine has long sought the ability to manufacture Patriot interceptors, which are costly and in high demand, as it defends against Russian air and missile attacks.

stories.headline: Taiwan's exports jump nearly 50 percent in first half on AI demand
stories.summary: Taiwan's exports rose nearly 50 percent in the first half of 2026, putting the economy on course for its fastest annual growth in more than a decade, Nikkei Asia reported. The report attributed the surge to Taiwan's role as a leading chip producer benefiting from global demand tied to the artificial intelligence boom, and said the government had raised its 2026 growth outlook to a 16-year high.
